flashcards.front: What is a constructor in C++?
flashcards.back: A special member function of a class that is automatically invoked when an object is created. It has the same name as the class and no return type.

flashcards.front: What are the properties of a constructor?
flashcards.back: Same name as class, no return type (not even void), automatically called on object creation, can be overloaded, must be declared in public (usually).
A constructor that takes no parameters or all parameters have default values. It is provided by compiler if no constructor is defined.
A constructor that accepts arguments to initialize data members with specific values. Enables object initialization with custom values.
A constructor that creates an object by copying another existing object of the same class. Takes reference to object as parameter.
Having multiple constructors in a class with different parameter lists (different number or types of parameters).

flashcards.front: What is a member initializer list?
flashcards.back: A syntax to initialize data members directly in constructor definition using colon after parameter list. More efficient than assignment.
A constructor marked with 'explicit' keyword to prevent implicit conversions from single-parameter constructor.
A constructor declared in private section. Used in Singleton design pattern and for factory methods.

flashcards.front: When is the copy constructor called?
flashcards.back: When: 1) Passing object by value to function, 2) Returning object by value, 3) Initializing one object with another, 4) Object is passed by value to constructor.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between constructor and destructor?
flashcards.back: Constructor initializes object (called when created), destructor cleans up (called when object goes out of scope). Constructor name = class name, destructor = ~classname.
A constructor that allocates memory dynamically using 'new' operator to initialize pointer data members.
No, constructors are not inherited. Derived class must define its own constructor to initialize its members and base class.

flashcards.front: What is the order of constructor execution in inheritance?
flashcards.back: Base class constructor executes first, then derived class constructor. Destructor executes in reverse order.
A C++11 feature where one constructor calls another constructor in the same class to initialize the object.
